stories.headline: US launches new strikes on Iran, targeting missile sites and boats
stories.summary: US Central Command says the strikes were taken in "self-defense". It comes as senior Iranian negotiators arrive in Qatar for talks to end the war.

stories.headline: Iran war live: US strikes Iran’s south, Tehran officials in Qatar for talks
stories.summary: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian has ordered the restoration of internet services after months of blocked access.

stories.headline: US strikes Iranian missile sites as peace talks continue
stories.summary: Washington announces ‘self-defence’ operation against targets in southern Iran

stories.headline: Oil Climbs as US Strikes Iran Targets | The Asia Trade 5/26/2026
stories.summary:
"Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: From tanks to drones: Ukraine's drone war is redefining Germany's battlefield strategy
stories.summary: Ukrainian drone commanders fighting on the front line say modern warfare has changed faster than Europe's armies can adapt. As Germany races to rearm, Kyiv's soldiers argue the future of war will be decided not just by tanks – but by drones, mass production and trained operators.

stories.headline: US strikes southern Iran in ‘self defence’ targeting Iranian mine boats
stories.summary: The US military says it has launched new strikes on southern Iran, targeting Iranian missile sites and boats.

stories.headline: US military launches strikes on southern Iran amid talks in Qatar
stories.summary: US Central Command announces 'self-defence' strikes as Tehran's top negotiators gather in Qatar for talks.

stories.headline: Iran to open Hormuz 30 days after US deal to end fighting: source
US military says it has launched new strikes in Iran, including on missile launch sites
stories.summary: According to a statement from the US Central Command, the strikes were carried out “to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces".

stories.headline: Russia threatens more Kyiv strikes and tells foreign nationals to leave
stories.summary: It comes after the Ukrainian capital suffered one of the biggest aerial assaults of the war overnight on Saturday.

stories.headline: Sasol’s Blistering Rally Meets Growing Skepticism From Analysts
stories.summary: After a rally that’s seen Sasol Ltd.’s stock price double this year, analysts are becoming wary of the South African oil and chemicals company.

stories.headline: Pressure Builds for Albanese Over Housing Tax Overhaul
stories.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is facing mounting public and political pressure following last week’s federal budget, which included proposed changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax (CGT) discount. Policy Institute of Australia CEO Amy Auster warns the tax changes could fail to address the core issue of housing supply. She spoke on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: China Coking Coal Prices Extend Gains After Shanxi Mine Disaster
stories.summary: Chinese coking coal futures pushed higher for a second session as investors watched for a broader government crackdown on the sector after a deadly accident at a mine in Shanxi province.

stories.headline: Australia politics live: Taylor pushes Albanese on small business CGT carve-out as two Coalition MPs ejected from question time
stories.summary: Opposition leader asks PM to declare which small businesses will receive a carve out from ‘broken promises and higher taxes’. stories.headline: Tensions rise in Bolivia as protesters march toward presidential palace
stories.summary: Demonstrators gathered near the presidential palace in La Paz as police maintained blockades and tensions remained high.

stories.headline: Fiber-optic drones add new dimension to Myanmar's civil war
The shrinking, monochrome future of Japan's war-ravaged food industry
Europe could fall into ‘dependency trap’ in AI trade with US and Asia, report finds
stories.summary: Europe relies on Asia for much of the infrastructure to power AI, and American companies hold large market shares in many tech fields, which could leave the continent at risk.

stories.headline: ‘Epstein class’ becomes a populist battle-cry in US politics
stories.summary: Accusations of elite impunity loom over November’s midterm elections

stories.headline: SpaceX to drive a cybertruck through corporate governance norms
stories.summary: Provisions in the IPO prospectus will give Elon Musk more freedom — and potentially less accountability

stories.headline: Deregulation hands top US and UK banks $1.3tn opening
stories.summary: Regulatory loosening has allowed countries’ banks to expand balance sheets while EU and Swiss rivals are constrained

stories.headline: Samsung’s Non-Chip Staff Seek Court Order to Block Wage Vote
stories.summary: A Samsung Electronics Co. union representing workers outside the ultra-profitable semiconductor division asked a Korean court to block voting on a tentative deal that would distribute about 40 trillion won ($26.6 billion) in bonuses to chip employees.

stories.headline: Korea to Monitor $37 Billion of Overseas Private Credit Exposure
stories.summary: South Korea will monitor overseas private debt investments from the nation that total some $37 billion, the government said, following recent scares in the industry globally.

stories.headline: Australian LNG Union Cancels Ichthys Strike as Talks Progress
stories.summary: Workers at Inpex Corp.’s Ichthys liquefied natural gas export project in Australia will not strike as planned on Wednesday and Thursday.

stories.headline: ASX Drops Most Since 2012 After Revising Up Costs on Tech Revamp
stories.summary: ASX Ltd. shares tumbled the most in more than a decade after the embattled exchange operator increased its capital spending guidance as it upgrades critical market infrastructure.

stories.headline: BOJ Deputy Chief Underlines Proper Policy as Key for Bond Yields
stories.summary: Bank of Japan Deputy Governor Ryozo Himino highlighted the importance of appropriate policy adjustments to keep the faith of market participants amid a recent selloff in government debt.

stories.headline: BHP ‘laughing’ at Australia’s key climate policy while pocketing hundreds of millions in tax breaks, Pocock says

stories.summary: Australia now a top-three global player in batteries, and renewable energy met nearly half of the nation’s power in 2025Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastEnergy bills for households and small businesses will fall by up to 10% from July across parts of the eastern states, as a new industry survey shows record levels of renewables and batteries in the power grid.Househol

stories.headline: Netanyahu vows to ‘smite’ Hezbollah with ‘overwhelming’ force
stories.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu vows to ‘smite’ Hezbollah with ‘overwhelming’ force.

stories.headline: Istanbul’s Bilgi University reopened after police crackdown
stories.summary: Students celebrated after days of protests against the closure decree that would have shut Bilgi University mid-year.

stories.headline: How AI is changing stock trades for retail investors
Hong Kong fire survivors face countdown on government buyout
India benefits from data center trade despite missing main AI play
Japan's extra budget won't add deficit-covering bonds, Takaichi says
Singapore Q1 GDP growth tops estimates at 6% on AI boom
Can heat pumps cool down your home and how do they compare to air conditioning units?
stories.summary: Europe consumes huge amounts of electricity and generates tonnes of CO2 through cooling. Could heat pumps save us during the next heatwave?

stories.headline: Where in Europe do households save most of their income?
stories.summary: Net household saving rates vary widely across Europe, with Greece being the only country where households spend more than they earn. Experts point to precautionary reasons and retirement as the dominant saving motives.

stories.headline: Iran is beating Trump at the art of the deal
stories.summary: The war could end with Tehran more confident, more hardline and with new resources to rebuild its nuclear programme

stories.headline: Russia urges foreign residents and diplomats to leave Kyiv as it threatens more strikes
stories.summary: One day after one of Russia's worst attacks on Kyiv since its 2022 full-scale invasion, Moscow warns foreign residents and diplomats to leave Ukraine's capital, as it says it does not intend on slowing down its attacks.

stories.headline: Trump tells US negotiators 'not to rush' into deal with Iran
stories.summary: The deal under discussion would involve a 60-day ceasefire extension during which the Strait of Hormuz would be reopened, according to US media.

stories.headline: Israel escalates Gaza attacks as Netanyahu stalls ceasefire for polls
stories.summary: Analysts warn Netanyahu is exploiting a failing "ceasefire" to prolong the destruction of Gaza ahead of Israeli election

stories.headline: Stocks Pare Gain, Oil Climbs as US Strikes Iran: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks trimmed gains and crude oil advanced after US strikes on sites in Iran curbed optimism over a potential deal to end the war.

stories.headline: Moment of gas explosion at China coal mine
stories.summary: At least 82 people have been killed and two are missing after a gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China on Sunday, officials say.

stories.headline: Trump pays tribute to US troops killed in war on Iran on Memorial Day
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump marked Memorial Day at Arlington National Cemetery, paying tribute to US troops killed.

stories.headline: Father mourns daughter killed in Israeli strike on Gaza
stories.summary: A six-year-old Palestinian girl was killed by an Israeli strike on a displacement camp in Khan Younis.

stories.headline: Trump Urges More Countries to Normalize Relations With Israel as Part of Iran Deal
stories.summary: President Trump called for countries in the Middle East to join the Abraham Accords as part of a peace deal with Iran. Analysts say it is unlikely.

stories.headline: Belarus opposition leader visits Kyiv after Russia's most devastating attack this year
stories.summary: Ukraine and its allies are increasingly concerned about Belarusian-Russian collaboration, while Moscow has warned that further strikes on Kyiv are coming.

stories.headline: Oil prices fall below $100 a barrel on hopes of Iran peace deal
stories.summary: Brent crude futures down 6% to lowest level in two weeks and stock markets riseOil prices fell below $100 a barrel on Monday and stock markets rose on hopes that the US and Iran are inching closer to a peace deal.Brent crude futures, the global oil benchmark, were down 6% to $97.43 a barrel, the lowest level in two weeks, with hopes that an agreement to end the near three-month US-Israeli war on Iran can be struck. Continue reading...

stories.headline: Trump dangles normalisation amid pro-Israel criticism of possible Iran deal
stories.summary: US president says he is 'mandatorily requesting' that regional countries establish formal relations with Israel.

stories.headline: Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO
stories.summary: Director general of World Health Organization urges neighbouring countries to take immediate actionThe World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak is outpacing response efforts and countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are at high risk from the disease.“We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment the epidemic is outpacing us,” said the WHO’s director-general, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, as

stories.headline: Two swimmers killed as French authorities warn of deadly riptides across Atlantic coastline
stories.summary: Warm weather across France attracted large crowds to the Gironde coastline in the southwest despite dangerous sea conditions. Authorities said over 30 people were rescued, while two others lost their lives after being caught in strong currents.

stories.headline: Senegal's leadership row mounts as parliament speaker resigns
stories.summary: Some speculate that El Malick Ndiaye stepped down so that ousted-PM Ousmane Sonko can take his place.

stories.headline: Carney warns Alberta separatists of Brexit-style regret
stories.summary: Canada’s prime minister says oil-rich province’s push for independence is a ‘dangerous bluff’

stories.headline: Treasury Curve Flashes Higher-for-Longer Warning Under Warsh
stories.summary: A key Treasury yield gap shrank to its tightest level in a year as traders ramped up bets the Federal Reserve may keep interest rates higher for longer under new chairman Kevin Warsh.

stories.headline: Venezuela inmates occupy prison roof and set fire to mattresses to highlight alleged abuse
stories.summary: Inmates at Barinas prison allege they were peacefully protesting when prison staff opened fire, leaving some woundedInmates at Venezuela’s western Barinas prison staged a protest on its roof on Sunday, piling flaming mattresses and calling for the removal of the facility’s director, whom they accused of overseeing guards as they shot unarmed prisoners.“We want justice. They are shooting us, the guards and the wardens,” a prisoner said in a video

stories.headline: Iran delegation in Qatar seeks deal on frozen assets and Hormuz blockade
stories.summary: A high-level Iranian delegation led by parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, FM Abbas Araghchi and central bank chief Abdolnaser Hemmati arrived in Doha on Monday to discuss US accords and the unfreezing of blocked assets, according to anonymous sources familiar with the talks.

stories.headline: Cambodia's former opposition leader receives royal pardon for 27-year sentence
stories.summary: Kem Sokha was serving a 27-year sentence on treason charges, which were widely derided as politically motivated.

stories.headline: Clashes as Venezuelan prisoners protest over alleged mistreatment
stories.summary: The prisoners insisted their protest was peaceful but that security forces deployed to quell it opened fire.

stories.headline: China Aluminum Market Braces for Launch of Guinea Export Curbs
stories.summary: China’s aluminum industry is preparing for significant disruption to its raw materials supplies after Guinea, the world’s biggest bauxite producer, said it’s finalizing plans to limit exports of the ore.

stories.headline: Trump’s Self-Indulgent Streak Deepens G.O.P. Fears He Is Risking Losses in Midterms
stories.summary: “The stupid stuff is killing our chances,” said a retiring Republican senator.

stories.headline: Far-right Elam party inspired by Golden Dawn makes gains in Cyprus election
stories.summary: Strongly anti-Turkish party doubles its seats although mainstream parties did not see vote crumble as predictedAn anti-immigrant far-right party, inspired by Greece’s defunct neo-Nazi Golden Dawn, made the biggest gains in Sunday’s parliamentary election in Cyprus.Elam, the Greek National People’s Front, which has pushed for the closure of checkpoints on the ethnically split island and is vociferously anti-Turkish, doubled its seats in the 56-mem

stories.headline: Bolivia’s president cuts salary in half amid protests
stories.summary: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz says he and his cabinet ministers will cut their salaries by 50 percent.

stories.headline: THE KISWA: BEYOND THE CLOTH
stories.summary: Every Muslim on earth has faced it in prayer. Billions have seen it in photos. Almost nobody knows its story.

stories.headline: Bolivia’s embattled president says will take a 50 percent salary cut
stories.summary: Rodrigo Paz says the salary cut for him and cabinet ministers shows the government's 'commitment to the country'.

stories.headline: Housing and climate resilience dominate Latin American agenda at WUF13
stories.summary: Latin American leaders at World Urban Forum 13 called for new housing finance models, stronger private investment and climate-resilient urban development.

stories.headline: MENA leaders call for housing to go beyond 'bricks and mortar' at Baku forum
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia, Palestine and Morocco took to the stage at WUF13 in Baku to argue that housing policy must go beyond construction — linking shelter to jobs, climate resilience and human dignity.

stories.headline: Qatar exports World Cup know-how as 2026 tournament approaches
stories.summary: As football fans gear up for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, Qatar is sharing its 2022 experience and knowledge in organising football's top tournament with its North American peers.

stories.headline: Man killed in shark attack off Australia's north-east coast
stories.summary: The 39-year-old was fatally injured at a shoal off the Cassowary Coast in Queensland, apparently while spearfishing.

stories.headline: Ebola outbreak poses massive challenges, warns nurse
stories.summary: Kate White says she is "extremely concerned about the inability to get resources" to the Democratic Republic of Congo.

stories.headline: Hunger increasingly used as weapon of war as ‘food-related violence’ surges, analysis shows
stories.summary: More than 20,000 attacks on markets, farmland and food distribution systems have been recorded since 2018Hunger is being increasingly exploited as a weapon of war with more than 20,000 documented incidents of “food-related violence” in the past eight years, new analysis reveals.Attacks include 1,261 strikes on markets used by families for daily groceries and 863 incidents in which food distribution systems were targeted and workers killed. Contin

stories.headline: Turkish riot police storm opposition offices after leaders ousted
stories.summary: Officers forced their way into the building days after the party vowed to defy a court ruling removing its leaders.

stories.headline: Blast targeting train kills at least 20 in Pakistan
stories.summary: Officials say the train had been transporting military personnel home for Eid when it was attacked by armed separatists.

stories.headline: Indian billionaires buy foreign companies as growth slows at home
stories.summary: India Inc spent $18bn on global buyouts in 2025 and the deal value could cross $15bn in the first half of 2026.

stories.headline: Bolivian minister's convoy ambushed while overseeing roadblock clearance
stories.summary: Security forces were met by resistance while attempting to clear highway barriers, amid unrest over the government's economic policies.

stories.headline: China mine death toll at least 82 after gas blast
stories.summary: Xi Jinping urges authorities to ‘spare no effort’ in rescue operation after explosion in northern Shanxi provinceAt least 82 people have been killed in a gas explosion at a coalmine in northern China’s Shanxi province, in the country’s worst mining disaster in 17 years.The explosion happened on Friday at 7.29pm (12.29 BST) while 247 workers were underground at the Liushenyu coalmine in Qinyuan county, the state media agency Xinhua reported. Conti

stories.headline: UK officials suggested single market for goods with Europe
stories.summary: Industry figures briefed on the move said the idea has not been taken forward at this point, after EU scepticism.

stories.headline: US arms sales to Taiwan on ‘pause’ due to Iran war, says acting navy chief
stories.summary: Hung Cao’s comments are latest blow to Taipei after Donald Trump recently cast doubt over US’s enduring supportUS arms sales to Taiwan have been “paused” to ensure the US military has enough munitions for its Iran operations, according to Washington’s acting navy secretary, in the latest blow to Taipei after a series of comments by Donald Trump.When asked at a congressional hearing on Thursday about a $14bn (£10.4bn) weapons package awaiting Trum

stories.headline: PM says Alberta ‘essential’ to Canada as separatists push for independence
stories.summary: Alberta premier calls for referendum on secession after judge ruled initiative to force binding vote invalid The Canadian prime minister, Mark Carney, has said that Alberta is “essential” to the country’s future, hours after the province’s leader moved the oil-rich region closer toward a referendum on independence.Separatists in the western province spent months collecting signatures seeking to trigger a binding October vote on seceding from the

stories.headline: UN’s climate crisis vote shows political momentum is growing, say experts
stories.summary: Resolution backed by 141 states hailed as ‘new chapter’ that could improve climate diplomacy and litigation effortsWhen the UN general assembly voted overwhelmingly in favour of a landmark climate crisis ruling on Wednesday, the Pacific island of Vanuatu’s prime minister hailed the result as the start of “a new chapter” in climate action.“The task before all of us now is to translate legal clarity into meaningful action, stronger cooperation, and

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Heavy rain triggers flooding and landslides in parts of China
stories.summary: Twelve dead as southern and central areas also suffer travel disruption, electricity outages and evacuationsHeavy, prolonged rainfall in southern and central China has resulted in weather warnings for flash flooding, landslides and waterlogging. The slow-moving band of rainfall spanned 620 miles (1,000km) and travelled eastwards across the regions on Tuesday. It was formed from the convergence of multiple bands of rain originating from the Bay of

stories.headline: Rubio tries to reassure Nato allies over US troop deployments
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says he wants to send more troops to Poland, a week after his officials cancelled a similar deployment.

stories.headline: Sabalenka cuts media short as French Open players protest
stories.summary: Leading players, including Aryna Sabalenka, Coco Gauff and Jannik Sinner, limit their French Open media duties to 15 minutes in a row over prize money.

stories.headline: 'No means no': Greenlanders protest against Trump outside new US consulate
stories.summary: The inauguration of the new consulate comes amid a push by the US president for greater control over the island.

stories.headline: ‘Long live Raúl!’ - Castro’s supporters in Cuba rally in his defence
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant reports from a pro-government demonstration held after the US indictment of the former president.

stories.headline: The deadly plane attack at the centre of Castro's indictment
stories.summary: Raúl Castro was armed forces minister when Cuban military jets shot down two civilian planes operated by exiles of the communist country.

stories.headline: Watch key moments from BBC's interview with pardoned Honduran ex-president
stories.summary: Juan Orlando Hernández was controversially pardoned by Trump in December while serving a 45-year prison sentence in the US for drug trafficking.
